author: misilva73
content: How can we watch the ACDT BAL breakout section? Is there a specific link/event?

author: raxhvl
content:
Can someone challenge this: I think our tests dont cover client "builder path" for BAL.

All our fixtures are generated using EEST as a builder. For clients, our tests hit the validation,
and NOT the builder path. I.e We test if clients accept a correct BAL and NOT if it builds correct one.

Im thinking what are the odds that we get a consensus issue because the client genrates a wrong bal,
but accepts a correct one.

It could be that the BAL generation code is shared between both paths. But, like we've been discussing,
tests shouldnt assume anything about client implementaiton. Besides, we alteast know one key difference - 
builder path is sequential and validator path is parallel. 

Maybe the risk is low - but i want to be sure we leave no gap.

Can we do a "build parity check" - by "filling" against ALL clients (NOT just EEST) and check if it results in the same fixture.

Perhaps we should do this for all tests that touch consensus by generating this "cannonical fixture".

author: nero_eth
content:
I just wanted to tag you 😄
Do you think it could make sense to do BAL / 8037 breakout, basically have both in the same breakout - just in case there's need - because there's not too much on today's BAL agenda anyway?

author: nero_eth
content: There will be a popup during the call allowing you to either enter the BAL breakout or the ePBS breakout  (so, there is no separate link), does this answer your question?

author: nero_eth
content:
This wouldn’t cause a real consensus issue, the block would just be invalid, get no attestations, and be reorged.
As long as all clients agree on validation, a bad builder path just means proposing bad blocks, not a chain split.

author: nero_eth
content: But agree, this must not happen as it could mean we lose liveness

author: qu0b
content: One could argue that the block building path can be left to the client to test

author: raxhvl
content:
I think this bug is exactly the kind - Nethermind's BAL had phantom entries. It computed BAL too early and ended up also picking rejected txs from mempool.

https://github.com/NethermindEth/nethermind/pull/10971
